title: Curations.DEV grounding snapshot
description: The public contract for versioned world-state snapshots (schema 0.1.0). A snapshot is what a grounding agent captured about a category's state of the art at a moment in time — the temporal axis of the moonshot dataset. Snapshot CONTENTS live in the private store (open-core split); this contract plus each snapshot's digest hash are public so citations are verifiable without exposing the corpus.

snapshot_ref.description: Stable reference, e.g. 'bing/2026-W30/devtools'. Cited by suggestion plans and run-records.

queries.description: The literal queries issued — versioned inputs, reproducible intent.

content_digest.description: Digest of the private snapshot content. Public verifiability without public contents.

spend.description: Bounded spend, recorded per snapshot. Exceeding the cap fails the capture — never silently.

previous_snapshot.description: Lineage to the prior snapshot for the same category set — trend deltas walk this chain exactly like run-record lineage.
